question_title: Will the interest in “cpac” change between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-11 according to Google Trends?
summary: CPAC 2026 peaked at 100 on March 28 during the conference and has been in rapid exponential decay since. By April 1, the value is already 14 — down 86% from peak in just 4 days (half-life ~1.5 days). Historical precedent from 2024 CPAC shows nearly identical decay: from 18 at day+4 to 3 at day+14. The baseline for "cpac" searches is 2-4. With the start value at 14 and the expected end value around 2-4, the expected decrease of ~10-12 points vastly exceeds the ±3 threshold. No CPAC events are scheduled in the April 1-11 window, and all conference news coverage concluded by March 31. The viral "impeachment cheers" moment was a conference-specific story, not an ongoing catalyst. "Decreases" is the overwhelmingly likely outcome.

factors.description: Post-conference exponential decay: CPAC 2026 peaked Mar 28 at 100, at 14 by Apr 1. Decay rate (k=0.47, half-life 1.5 days) projects near-baseline (2-4) by Apr 11. In 2024, day+4 value of 18 decayed to 3 by day+14.
Current value (14) is well above baseline (2-4). The 10-12 point gap to baseline far exceeds the ±3 threshold. Even partial decay to 7-8 qualifies as Decreases.

No scheduled CPAC events April 1-11. Conference ended March 29. All major news coverage (NPR, CNN, Slate) published March 28-31. The impeachment cheers story was a viral conference moment, not ongoing.

Small possibility of external catalyst: renewed political attention to CPAC impeachment discussion, or major CPAC figure in news. Speculative — would need to drive 'cpac'-specific searches, not just general impeachment interest.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement may differ by 1-3 points, but expected 10-12 point change provides large buffer over ±3 threshold.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (88%)**

Key reasoning:
- **CPAC 2026 is in rapid post-conference decay.** Interest peaked at 100 on March 28 and has already fallen to 14 by April 1 (half-life ~1.5 days). By April 11, it should be near the baseline of 2-4.
- **Historical precedent is decisive.** In 2024, CPAC interest decayed from 18 (day+4) to 3 (day+14) — a drop of 15 points. In 2025, the decay was also large (-17 points over the equivalent window).
- **The expected change (~10-12 points) vastly exceeds the ±3 threshold** needed to resolve as "Decreases."
- **No catalysts exist** in the April 1-11 window — no scheduled CPAC events, and all conference news coverage wrapped up by March 31.

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| Decreases | 88% |
| Doesn't change | 9% |
| Increases | 3% |
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=cpac&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=cpac&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11) for 2026-04-11 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-01. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "cpac",
  "date": "2026-03-12 2026-04-11",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
